---
title: "Stratified HUMAnN Co-Expression Analysis"
author: "Auto-generated by Snakemake pipeline"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: cosmo
    code_folding: hide
params:
  outdir: "results"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.align = "center")
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    library(tidyverse)
    library(data.table)
    library(DT)
    library(knitr)
})
OUT <- params$outdir
```

# Overview

This report summarizes the **species-stratified functional co-expression** analysis produced by the Snakemake pipeline.

## Preprocessing summary

```{r preprocess}
summary_tbl <- fread(file.path(OUT, "preprocess/preprocess_summary.tsv"))
DT::datatable(summary_tbl, options = list(pageLength = 20))
```

## Functional redundancy

Functional redundancy quantifies how many distinct taxa contribute to each function in each sample. High redundancy indicates ecological robustness; low redundancy flags vulnerable functions.

```{r redundancy_table}
red_sum <- fread(file.path(OUT, "redundancy/redundancy_summary.tsv"))
DT::datatable(head(red_sum, 50), options = list(pageLength = 20))
```

![Specialist vs generalist functions](`r file.path(OUT, "redundancy/plot_specialist_vs_generalist.pdf")`)

## WGCNA function co-expression modules

Module assignments group functions that vary together across samples. Module-trait correlations identify modules linked to treatment.

```{r wgcna_modules}
mods <- fread(file.path(OUT, "wgcna/module_assignment.tsv"))
cat("Number of modules:", length(unique(mods$module)), "\n")
DT::datatable(mods[, .N, by = module][order(-N)], caption = "Module sizes")
```

```{r wgcna_corr}
tcor <- fread(file.path(OUT, "wgcna/module_trait_correlation.tsv"))
DT::datatable(tcor, caption = "Module-trait correlations")
```

![Module dendrogram](`r file.path(OUT, "wgcna/plot_module_dendrogram.pdf")`)

![Module-trait heatmap](`r file.path(OUT, "wgcna/plot_module_trait_heatmap.pdf")`)

## Bipartite taxon-function network

```{r bipartite_metrics}
bm <- fread(file.path(OUT, "bipartite/specialization_metrics.tsv"))
DT::datatable(head(bm, 30), caption = "Top specialization metrics")
```

![Bipartite network](`r file.path(OUT, "bipartite/plot_bipartite_network.pdf")`)

## DIABLO integrative analysis

Sparse PLS-DA integrating taxa and functions against treatment group. Selected loadings indicate features driving group separation jointly across blocks.

```{r diablo_loadings}
loads <- fread(file.path(OUT, "diablo/diablo_loadings.tsv"))
DT::datatable(loads, caption = "Selected features (block, feature, loading)")
```

![DIABLO scores](`r file.path(OUT, "diablo/diablo_scores.pdf")`)

![Circos plot of cross-block correlations](`r file.path(OUT, "diablo/plot_circos.pdf")`)

## Headline summary (annotated top features)

Top features per analysis with human-readable KEGG names where available. This is the table to look at first.

```{r headline}
hs <- fread(file.path(OUT, "top_features_summary/headline_summary.tsv"))
DT::datatable(hs, caption = "Top finding per analysis", options = list(pageLength = 10))
```

```{r top_modules}
tm <- fread(file.path(OUT, "top_features_summary/top_modules.tsv"))
DT::datatable(tm, caption = "Top WGCNA modules by treatment correlation")
```

```{r top_kegg_paths}
tk <- fread(file.path(OUT, "top_features_summary/top_kegg_pathways.tsv"))
DT::datatable(tk, caption = "Top KEGG pathway enrichments per module")
```

## KEGG pathway enrichment per WGCNA module

For KO-level inputs, each WGCNA module is tested for KEGG pathway enrichment via Fisher's exact test. For COG-level inputs, this section is skipped automatically.

```{r kegg}
kegg <- fread(file.path(OUT, "kegg_enrichment/module_pathway_table.tsv"))
DT::datatable(head(kegg, 50), caption = "Top KEGG pathway enrichments per module")
```

![Module x pathway -log10(FDR) heatmap](`r file.path(OUT, "kegg_enrichment/plot_module_pathway_heatmap.pdf")`)

## ANCOM-BC2 differential abundance

Compositional differential abundance testing (ANCOM-BC2) for functions and taxa between treatment groups.

```{r ancom_func}
af <- fread(file.path(OUT, "ancombc/ancombc_function_results.tsv"))
DT::datatable(head(af, 50), caption = "Top differentially abundant functions")
```

![Volcano plot — functions](`r file.path(OUT, "ancombc/plot_volcano_function.pdf")`)

```{r ancom_taxa}
at <- fread(file.path(OUT, "ancombc/ancombc_taxa_results.tsv"))
DT::datatable(head(at, 50), caption = "Top differentially abundant taxa")
```

![Volcano plot — taxa](`r file.path(OUT, "ancombc/plot_volcano_taxa.pdf")`)

## Procrustes test

Tests whether taxonomic and functional sample-distance structures are concordant. A significant test means taxa and functions track together; a non-significant test reveals functional redundancy.

```{r procrustes}
pr <- fread(file.path(OUT, "procrustes/procrustes_test.tsv"))
DT::datatable(pr, caption = "Procrustes test")
```

![Procrustes superposition](`r file.path(OUT, "procrustes/plot_procrustes.pdf")`)

# Citations

Please cite Snakemake, HUMAnN3, WGCNA, mixOmics, bipartite, and vegan if you publish from this output. See README for exact references.
